---
title: ""
description: "."
---


# RSL reference

RSL (Rustimate Studio Language) is the language used to describe the outcome. 


An RSL file defines scenes, objects, timeline events, transitions, and visual properties.  
Think of it as a sequence of instructions an, object declarations.

::: {.callout-tip}
##  RSL compiler coming in 2027
Native RSL compiler is in development. The current interpreter handles the full feature set described here.
:::


# Scenes 

This is the basic building block of RSL. It contains the keyword scene with a scene name.
Scenes define logical sections of the visualization. A single `.rsl` file can contain multiple scenes.  


```bash
scene "scene_name" {

}
```

::: {.callout-tip}
##  Syntax checking
The RSL syntax checking and errors will improve in the upcoming Rustimate versions among many other exciting features.
:::


# Components

Inside every scene, there are many components. It ranges from mode, animation, editor etc.
This section has detailed information of the components

## Mode - Presentation

In presentation mode, v0.1.0, any sub-components add will use a black background.

```bash
scene "intro" {
  mode: presentation
}
```

### Text 

While presentation mode renders any sub-components added on it but the best way is to add many text blocks you want.

```bash
scene "intro" {
  mode: presentation
  text "Hello world"
  text "Rustimate"
}
```

You can also add use text like this though I would suggest you use terminal mode which we will discuss below

```bash
scene "intro" {
text "fn main() {"
    text "    println!(\"Hello from Rustimate\");"
    text "}"

}
```

### Rustimate Guide

![](images/presentation_code.mp4){fig-align="center"}


### Animation

There are two values used by Animation which are `static` & `typewriter`. Static is default value.


```bash
scene "intro" {
  mode: presentation
  animation: typewriter
  text "Hello world"
  text "Rustimate"
}
```

### Rustimate Guide 

![](images/presentation_animation.mp4){fig-align="center"}


### Transition

Transition currently have one value called `fade`

```bash
scene "intro" {
  mode: presentation
  animation: typewriter
  text "Hello world"
  text "Rustimate"
}

scene "fade" {
  transition: fade
  mode: presentation
  text "fade effect"
}
```

### Rustimate Guide

![](images/presentation_fade.mp4){fig-align="center"}


## Mode - Editor

Rustimate can recreate real developer editor environments as the visual backdrop. It does not rendering the IDE itself.
The default theme is `monokai`

You can use this code to explain part of your code. Editor mode will help 

```bash
scene “scene2” {
    mode: editor
    editor: neovim
}
```

## Rustimate Neovim

![](images/neovim_ide.mp4){fig-align="center"}


```bash
scene “scene2” {
    mode: editor
    editor: emacs
}
```
## Rustimate emacs

![](images/emacs_ide.mp4){fig-align="center"}



## Editor Themes

Themes control the colour system and visual style of the entire scene.  
Rustimate `v0.1.0` uses three themes for both editors

* Monokai (default)
* Dracula
* Nord


```bash
scene “scene2” {
    mode: editor
    editor: emacs/neovim
    theme: nord/dracula/monokai
}
```

::: {.callout-tip}
##  Themes
More themes and additional editor support will be added soon.
:::


### Nord Theme
This is how the nord theme would look like in emacs. 

![](images/emacs_ide_nord.mp4){fig-align="center"}


### Dracula Theme
This is how the dracula theme would look like in Neovim 

![](images/neovim_ide_dracula.mp4){fig-align="center"}



## Files

You can render specific lines of a real code file using theme and editor:

```bash
scene "Code" {
  mode: editor
  editor: neovim
  animation: typewriter
  file: "main.rs"
}
```

## Rustimate Guide

![](images/file_editor.mp4){fig-align="center"}



##  Lines

Imagine you want to render only specific lines from the *main.rs* file. This is where `lines` are quite helpful

```bash
scene "Code" {
  mode: editor
  editor: neovim
  animation: typewriter
  file: "main.rs"
  lines: 5..8
}
```

## Rustimate Guide

![](images/file_editor_lines.mp4){fig-align="center"}


## Highlight

What if during your explaination, you want to highlight important or specific lines of code.
This is where `highlight` comes into play.

The below rsl file will help you extract lines from 5 to 8 and highlight line 6.
And yes, you can include the animation too!


```bash
scene "Code" {
  mode: editor
  animation: typewriter
  editor: neovim
  animation: typewriter
  file: "main.rs"
  lines: 5..8
  highlight: [6]
}
```


## Rustimate Guide
![](images/file_editor_highlight.mp4){fig-align="center"}
